mechanism_narrative: Persistent low levels of the federal funds rate compress discount rates, increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 20-day returns regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with FEDFUNDS level is not significantly negative (Pearson r > -0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7%, recession fears dominate market sentiment, causing immediate selling pressure and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances (UNRATE>7%), the median 5-day forward return is non-negative or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50% over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation (CPIAUCSL yoy > 3%) and low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL inflation) produce deeply negative real interest rates, signaling imminent monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL yoy > 3% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL yoy
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following trigger is not negative, or the difference in mean returns between trigger and non-trigger periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10) over two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, above-trend GDP growth (real GDP quarterly annualized > 2%) signals overheating that forces the Federal Reserve into tightening mode, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward periods classified as late-cycle, 60-day forward returns following trigger are positive more than 50% of the time, or the long-short return spread is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which flows through to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month change in PAYEMS and subsequent 20-day return is below 0.05 (absolute value) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail and food services sales exceeding 1% signals a sudden consumer demand contraction, triggering immediate risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following trigger events, 5-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time, or the average return is within one standard deviation of unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous deterioration of the labor market (UNRATE rising >0.5 percentage points over 3 months) and industrial production (INDPRO declining over 3 months) constitutes a classic recession signal that drives sustained equity losses over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return following joint trigger is not below the unconditional median return by at least 2 percentage points on average over all historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, any increase in the federal funds rate from the previous month intensifies expected borrowing cost headwinds, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles (FOMC rate hikes), the average 20-day return after a rate increase is not negative, or the hit rate (negative returns) is below 60%.
